channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Over 1,000 high school students across San Diego County walk out for environment
item.description: More than 1,000 students across 19 high schools in San Diego County participated in a state-wide walkout on Friday, urging lawmakers to pass climate related legislation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Trump heads to 3-country sprint in Asia, meeting with Xi as government shutdown drags on at home
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia Friday night for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to deescalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dallas pediatrician sued over providing hormone treatments to teens surrenders medical license
item.description: After Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued last year, Dr. May Lau said she is moving to Oregon and doesn't need a Texas license anymore.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Xiomara Moore, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is rejecting the idea of using roughly $5 billion in contingency funds to keep food aid flowing into November amid the government shutdown, according to a Department of Agriculture memo that surfaced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries backs Mamdani for NYC mayor months after primary
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries on Friday endorsed Zohran Mamdani for mayor of New York City, saying he respected the will of the primary voters and young people inspired by the Democratic socialist state lawmaker's candidacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing a volatile mix of blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to jet off to Asia as North Korea fires ballistic missiles and China trade questions loom
item.description: Trump's Asia summit tour includes meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping and other regional leaders as trade tensions with Beijing escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel Democrats press Gabbard on Trump administration boat strikes
item.description: Leading Intelligence Committee Democrats are accusing the Trump administration of leaving them in the dark on strikes in the Caribbean that have killed more than 40 people, demanding information about the intelligence community’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ sends federal election watchers to California and New Jersey counties amid Republican requests
item.description: Democrats clash with Trump's DOJ over federal election observers in California and New Jersey, calling deployment an 'intimidation tactic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama’s ‘wingman’ dumps $300K into new Virginia redistricting fight as Dems call lawmakers back
item.description: Virginia Democrats surprise lawmakers with Monday special session on redistricting, sparking Republican accusations of a 'power grab' in Richmond.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Qatari embassy official reveals Biden admin role in new Idaho training facility finalized under Trump
item.description: A senior Qatari embassy official outlined the details of the controversial Qatari Emiri Air Force facility at the Mountain Home Air Base in Idaho.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump and Kim Jong Un should make 'bold decision' to meet during his Asia trip, South Korean official says
item.description: South Korea's unification minister Chung Dong-young urges President Donald Trump and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to meet during Trump's trip to Asia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mamdani laughs off criticism that his tax hike proposals will compel New Yorkers to flee
item.description: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani laughed about concerns over his proposed tax hikes during a podcast discussion about whether they would cause residents to flee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transportation chief warns of travel impacts amid shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy has said the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey online portal helps residents claim lost funds
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jerseyans, did you know that you can check if you have unclaimed money in the Garden State? The New Jersey Unclaimed Property Administration has an online portal that residents can utilize to search for property they may be owed.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Live: Lawmakers hear from witnesses on Trump actions in Chicago
item.description: Witnesses are expected to testify to their personal experiences, trauma, alleged constitutional violations and labor impacts.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security cost-of-living increase will go up by 2.8% in 2026, which translates to an average increase of more than $56 for retirees every month, agency officials said Friday. The benefits increase will go into effect for Social Security recipients beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SNAP pause could harm families, say county workers
item.description: Minnesota officials say residents who get food assistance won’t receive their benefits for November, as long as the federal shutdown continues. County workers say they’re now hearing from concerned enrollees in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Inflation hits 3% in first report since shutdown
item.description: The data comes more than a week late, after the Bureau of Labor called back furloughed employees to finish it.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration uses shutdown to push Education Department elimination
item.description: The Education Department is trying to use the government shutdown to further push for its own elimination.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: SNAP emerges as flash point in shutdown fight
item.description: The battle over food stamps has emerged as the latest flash point in the fierce partisan clash over government spending.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: NY AG Letitia James to make first court appearance in mortgage fraud case
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James is set to make her first court appearance in a mortgage fraud case on Friday, the third adversary of President Donald Trump to face a judge on federal charges in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here's what the White House lost in East Wing demolition
item.description: Demolition of the White House's East Wing, itself a source of multiple controversies for over 200 years, was completed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives disaster declarations to Alaska and others but denies Illinois, Vermont and Maryland
item.description: President Donald Trump approved major disaster declarations for Alaska, Nebraska, North Dakota and the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe late Wednesday, while denying requests from Vermont, Illinois and Maryland and leaving other states still waiting for answers.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What lawmakers are saying about Trump's demolition of the East Wing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — They're divided along party lines on policy. They're divided on the government shutdown. And now federal lawmakers are divided on the demolition of the East Wing of the White House to make room for the 90,000-square-foot ballroom that President Donald Trump wants to build.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate rejects bills to pay federal workers during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Thursday rejected dueling partisan bills to pay federal workers during the government shutdown, with both Republicans and Democrats deflecting blame as many employees are set to miss their first full paycheck at the end of this week.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Senate's votes on pay for federal workers fail as shutdown continues
item.description: Air traffic controllers and military service members are among those who are working without pay during the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Texas book-rating law ruled unconstitutional by federal court for 3rd time
item.description: The two-year long legal battle over a Texas law will draw to an end after a federal court judge ruled Tuesday that the law violates the US Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from the final New York City mayor's debate
item.description: There was no shortage of fireworks between Democrat Zohran Mamdani, independent Andrew Cuomo and Republican Curtis Sliwa.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Preservation group urges pause in White House East Wing demolition
item.description: The destruction of a portion of the White House’s East Wing to create the ballroom has hit a nerve among critics.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats block government funding bill for 12th time after marathon floor protest
item.description: Senate Democrats voted Wednesday for a 12th time to block a House-passed bill to reopen the government and keep it funded through Nov. 21. Democrats blocked the funding measure — a clean continuing resolution that would prolong Biden-era funding levels — after liberal Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Judge agrees to extend block on Guard deployment in Chicago while awaiting Supreme Court ruling
item.description: As the U.S. Supreme Court weighs whether to clear the way for the National Guard in Chicago, a federal judge on Wednesday agreed to extend a two-week block on Guard deployment in the Chicago area by 30 days.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What the federal government shutdown means countrywide
item.description: The federal government remains shut down. The NPR Network is following the ways the shutdown is affecting services across the country.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
